title: Batch 1 Auditor Pre-Approval — 25 Proposed Loan Pages
batch_reviewed: Original Batch 1: 25 loan payment pages from first-100-page-test-batch.json

audit_summary.cannibalization_risk: LOW — loan database targets specific long-tail, not head term 'loan calculator'
audit_summary.template_uniqueness: FAIL — all 25 pages use identical template with swapped numbers
audit_summary.demand_evidence_quality: MIXED — 1 page has exact GSC match (20K at 8%), others have autocomplete or generic patterns
audit_summary.build_complexity: LOW — single template, parameterized. Easy to build technically.
audit_summary.internal_link_safety: PASS — all pages link to parent loan-calculator. No orphan risk.
audit_summary.expected_indexing_probability: LOW — near-duplicate pages with thin differentiation unlikely to index well

verdict_explanation: Batch 1 as proposed (25 near-identical loan pages) CANNOT be built. 24/25 pages fail quality gate and show doorway/duplication risk. The batch must be significantly restructured before any build or deploy.
what_must_change: REDUCE VARIANT COUNT: Select 3-5 strongest loan variants instead of 25. Choose those with highest demand evidence and most differentiation potential.
ADD UNIQUE CONTENT PER PAGE: Each page must have 3+ unique content blocks not shared with siblings. Real-world scenarios, credit score guidance, comparison tables specific to each variant.

DIFFERENTIATE BY USE CASE: Rather than number-swapping, create pages for specific USE CASES: 'Small Personal Loan ($5K at 15%)', 'Debt Consolidation Loan ($25K at 10%)', 'Auto Loan ($20K at 7%)', 'Home Improvement Loan ($30K at 8%)'
RICH CONTEXT REQUIRED: Each page needs: real-world scenario, comparison table, credit score context, total cost breakdown, actionable next steps.
HAND-CRAFT DON'T MASS-PRODUCE: At 90/100 quality, we cannot template our way to volume. Each page must be individually crafted.

pages.differentiation: Focus on small loans with higher rates. Compare to payday loan alternatives. Explain why rates are higher for small amounts.
GSC-validated demand. Focus on what 'good credit' means. Compare 8% to average 12% rate. Show savings from good credit.
Focus on debt consolidation scenario. Compare to credit card APR. Show how consolidation saves money. Include snowball vs consolidation comparison.
Focus on auto loan or large purchase context. Compare to dealer financing. Show total vehicle cost including interest.
Focus on what happens with lower credit scores. Compare 20% to 8% — show the $6,000+ difference. Credit improvement tips.

each_page_must_have: Unique use case and real-world scenario
Comparison table with at least 2 alternative scenarios
Credit score or rate context specific to this tier
Total cost breakdown with interest as percentage of principal
At least 1 unique warning or tip
Distinct title, meta, and H1 from all siblings

what_happens_next.if_restructured_batch_approved: Build 5 pages. Score each. If all pass 90+, deploy as controlled test batch.
what_happens_next.if_restructured_batch_rejected: Further refine. Do not deploy until approved.
what_happens_next.after_batch_deploy: Monitor indexing for 14-21 days. Measure: indexed rate, impressions, clicks. Decision: scale (add more pages) or hold (fix quality).

do_not_deploy_until: All 5 restructured pages pass 90/100 quality gate + doorway audit = SAFE + Auditor final approval.
